Swiss gold producers interested in setting up in US, says official
data.description:
Swiss gold producers are interested in setting up shop in the United States in the future, said a senior Swiss economic affairs official on Friday at a press conference after a tariff agreement was reached with Washington.

Gold, which is a large contributor to Switzerland's trade surplus with the United States, remains exempt from tariffs, according.

However, gold is not very profitable for Switzerland, with a "very, very small margin of perhaps 1% or less," said Helene Budliger Artieda, director of the State Secretariat for Economic Affairs (SECO).

"But I believe it's important for them. For the U.S., it's crucial to strengthen its gold market," Budliger added.

The United States will reduce its tariffs on goods from Switzerland to 15% from a crippling 39% under a new framework trade agreement that includes a pledge by Swiss companies to invest $200 billion into the U.S. by the end of 2028, the Swiss government said on Friday.

data.title: Oklahoma County to Sell $25 Million in Single-Family Mortgage Revenue Bonds
data.description:
By Chris Wack

The Cleveland County Home Loan Authority plans to sell $25 million in single-family mortgage revenue bonds.

The Series 2025 A bonds are expected to be used to finance the purchase of single-family residential housing and the purchase of second-lien mortgage loans, according to a document posted Thursday on MuniOS.

Interest on the bonds will be payable starting on July 1 and again on Jan. 1. Interest rates and yields have not yet been set. The bonds mature from 2027 through 2056.

The bonds are being issued to provide financing for mortgage loans to be made to eligible borrowers for the purchase of newly constructed and existing single-family residences located within the geographic limits of Cleveland County, Oklahoma, and is just south of Oklahoma City. They will also be used for financing second-lien mortgage loans to such borrowers in order to provide downpayment and closing cost assistance.

The bonds are special obligations of the authority, and aren't secured by, or payable from, the second-lien mortgage loans.

The authority is a duly constituted public trust, organized for the purpose of providing financing for the construction, acquisition and rehabilitation of homes.

Moody's assigned the bonds a rating of Aa1.

Stifel is the lead underwriter.

Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com

data.title: RESERVE PETROLEUM CO SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Reserve Petroleum Co., a leading player in the oil and gas industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational achievements, driven by increased natural gas sales and effective cost management. The company continues to optimize its production and streamline operations to sustain profitability amidst fluctuating market conditions.

Financial Highlights

Total Operating Revenues: $12.27 million, increased by 5% from the comparable period in 2024 due to higher natural gas sales.

Income from Operations: $4.72 million, reflecting a 34% increase driven by higher revenues and cost management.

Net Income: $4.32 million, up 23% from the previous year, attributed to increased natural gas sales and cost efficiencies.

Net Income Attributable to Common Stockholders: $4.34 million, a 23% increase from the prior year, with a significant contribution from natural gas sales.

Net Income Attributable to Common Stockholders, Basic: $28.62 per share, up from $22.89 per share in 2024, reflecting improved profitability.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company's oil and gas sales increased by 10% in the nine months ended September 30, 2025, driven by a significant 87% increase in natural gas sales, which offset a 5% decrease in oil sales. This growth was attributed to higher volumes and improved pricing for natural gas.

Sales Units: The volume of oil sold increased by 17,564 barrels to 135,757 barrels, while the volume of natural gas sold increased by 230,759 MCF to 951,739 MCF in the nine months ended September 30, 2025. This reflects a strong operational performance in terms of production and sales.

Operational Efficiency: The company achieved a gain on the sale of unproved, non-producing leasehold of $764,805, indicating effective asset management and optimization of its property portfolio.

Cost Management: Operating costs and expenses decreased by 7% to $7,550,423, with significant reductions in exploration costs and the elimination of water well drilling costs following the termination of the TWS Agreement.

Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued fluctuations in spot market prices for oil and natural gas, which will impact future sales and revenue. The company remains focused on optimizing production and managing costs to sustain profitability.

Operational Adjustments: The termination of the TWS Agreement resulted in the cessation of water well drilling operations, which previously contributed to revenue. This strategic decision is expected to streamline operations and reduce future expenses.

Asset Retirement Obligation: The company recorded an increase in its asset retirement obligation, reflecting ongoing commitments to environmental and regulatory compliance in its oil and gas operations.

SEC Filing: RESERVE PETROLEUM CO [ RSRV ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Propanc Biopharma, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Propanc Biopharma, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing new cancer treatments, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter ended September 30, 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities, highlighting significant challenges and future outlooks in the development of its lead product candidate, PRP.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0, The company reported no revenue for the three months ended September 30, 2025.
  • Loss from Operations: $(4.67) million, The company experienced a significant increase in operating expenses, primarily due to administration expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(4.84) million, The net loss increased substantially compared to the same period in 2024, driven by higher operating expenses and interest expenses.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Share: $(0.39), Reflects the impact of increased net loss on a larger number of shares outstanding compared to the prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Nature of Operations: Propanc Biopharma, Inc. is focused on developing new cancer treatments targeting high-risk patients, particularly cancer survivors, with a follow-up, non-toxic, long-term therapy designed to prevent cancer recurrence and spread. The lead product candidate, PRP, is in the preclinical phase of development.
  • Patent Portfolio: The company has a robust patent portfolio with 85 granted, allowed, or accepted patents and 5 patents filed or under examination in key global jurisdictions. These patents relate to the use of proenzymes against solid tumors, covering the lead product candidate PRP.
  • Geographical Presence: Propanc Biopharma, Inc. is based in Camberwell, Victoria, Australia, with a wholly-owned subsidiary in the UK, although the UK subsidiary remains inactive as of the reporting date.
  • Future Outlook: The company aims to establish global markets for its technologies and is focused on advancing its lead product candidate, PRP, through various stages of development. The company hopes to capture and protect additional patentable subject matter as it progresses.
  • Research and Development Focus: The company continues to invest in research and development, with expenses reported for the three months ended September 30, 2025, indicating ongoing efforts to advance its product pipeline.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faces significant challenges, including the need for additional financing to continue its development activities and achieve a level of sales adequate to support its cost structure and business plan. There is substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern.

SEC Filing: Propanc Biopharma, Inc. [ PPCB ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Lazydays Holdings, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Lazydays Holdings, Inc., a prominent player in the recreational vehicle (RV) retail industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report reveals significant financial and operational challenges faced by the company, including a substantial decline in revenue and profitability, as well as strategic decisions aimed at addressing these issues.

Financial Highlights

The financial performance of Lazydays Holdings, Inc. for the three months ended September 30, 2025, reflects a challenging period for the company:

  • Total Revenue: $101.4 million, a significant decrease from $205.9 million in the same period in 2024, primarily due to lower sales in new and pre-owned vehicle retail.
  • Gross Profit: $19.6 million, down from $45.3 million in the same period in 2024, indicating a decline in profitability.
  • Loss from Operations: $(79.2) million, compared to a loss of $(5.7) million in the same period in 2024, largely driven by impairment charges and increased selling, general, and administrative expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(82.4) million, compared to a net loss of $(17.7) million in the same period in 2024, reflecting the impact of impairment charges and other expenses.
  • Basic Loss Per Share: $(21.99), compared to $(41.19) in the same period in 2024, adjusted for the reverse stock split effective July 11, 2025.
  • Diluted Loss Per Share: $(21.99), reflecting the same adjustments as basic loss per share.

Business Highlights

The operational performance of Lazydays Holdings, Inc. during the third quarter of 2025 highlights several key challenges and strategic decisions:

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue from various segments including new vehicle retail, pre-owned vehicle retail, vehicle wholesale, consignment vehicle, finance and insurance, and service, body and parts. Each segment showed a decline in revenue compared to the previous year, reflecting a challenging market environment.
  • Geographical Performance: Florida and Tennessee were the primary contributors to revenue, with Florida accounting for 42% and Tennessee for 16% of total revenue in the third quarter of 2025. Arizona and Colorado also contributed, though less significantly.
  • Sales Units: The company experienced a decrease in the number of units sold across all vehicle categories, including new and pre-owned vehicles, which impacted overall sales performance.
  • Future Outlook: The company is in the process of selling its assets to CIRV Group, LLC and CIRV Group Real Estate Holdings, LLC, with the expectation that the proceeds will be used to repay outstanding obligations. The completion of these asset sales is anticipated to occur between November 17, 2025, and November 26, 2025.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faced significant operational challenges, including a net loss of $116.5 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, and substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern. The company has entered into waivers and consents to temporarily alleviate certain financial covenants.
  • Strategic Decisions: The company has decided to delist from Nasdaq and is planning a liquidation and dissolution following the completion of asset sales, as approved by the majority of its stockholders.
  • Management's Focus: Management is focused on completing the asset sales and managing the company's liquidity to sustain operations through the final closing of these sales.

SEC Filing: Lazydays Holdings, Inc. [ GORV ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Bristol-Myers Squibb Worst Performer in the S&P 500 So Far Today — Data Talk
data.description:
Bristol-Myers Squibb Company (BMY) is currently at $46.74, down $1.90 or 3.9%

  • On pace for largest percent decrease since July 31, 2025, when it fell 5.81%
  • Currently down two consecutive days; down 4.78% over this period
  • Worst two day stretch since the two days ending July 31, 2025, when it fell 7.58%
  • Up 0.10% this week; best weekly performance since the week ending Nov. 7, 2025, when it rose 1.35%
  • Up 1.44% month-to-date
  • Down 17.37% year-to-date; on pace for worst year since 2023, when it fell 28.69%
  • Down 42.39% from its all-time closing high of $81.13 on Dec. 2, 2022
  • Down 16.87% from 52 weeks ago (Nov. 15, 2024), when it closed at $56.22
  • Down 25.95% from its 52-week closing high of $63.11 on March 10, 2025
  • Up 9.71% from its 52-week closing low of $42.60 on Oct. 29, 2025
  • Traded as low as $46.12; lowest intraday level since Nov. 6, 2025, when it hit $45.96
  • Down 5.16% at today's intraday low; largest intraday percent decrease since July 31, 2025, when it fell as much as 6.57%
  • Worst performer in the S&P 500 today

All data as of 11:54:27 AM ET

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: REG - JPMorgan India G&I - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 6703H JPMorgan India Growth & Income PLC 14 November 2025  

LONDON STOCK EXCHANGE ANNOUNCEMENT

JPMORGAN INDIA GROWTH & INCOME PLC

REPURCHASE OF ORDINARY SHARES FOR TREASURY STOCK

Legal Entity Identifier: 549300OHW8R1C2WBYK02

JPMorgan India Growth & Income plc (the "Company") has today repurchased 10,000 ordinary shares into Treasury at 1039.3041 pence per share.

Following the transaction, the Company holds 34,733,382 ordinary shares in Treasury. The shares in issue less the total number of  Treasury shares is 45,062,123. The Company will only re-issue shares held in Treasury at a premium to net asset value.          

14th November 2025

For further information, please contact:

Sachu Saji

JPMorgan Funds Ltd

0800 20 40 20 (or +44 1268 44 44 70)

data.description: newsfile.refinitiv.com/getnewsfile/v1/story

data.title: FTSE 100 Suffers Worst Day since April
data.description:
The FTSE 100 fell 1.1% on Friday, marking its worst daily drop since April and nearly wiping out weekly gains, as UK gilt yields rose and the pound weakened amid reports that Chancellor Rachel Reeves may abandon planned income-tax hikes in the November 26 budget.

Concerns over the UK’s fiscal outlook prompted money markets to scale back expectations for Bank of England rate cuts to under 60 bps by the end of 2025.

Banks led declines, with NatWest down 3.5%, Barclays 3.1%, Lloyds and Standard Chartered over 2%, and HSBC down 1.5%.

Other major stocks also fell, including Unilever, Rolls-Royce, and British American Tobacco, which dropped between 1.4% and 1.9%.
